response:
for the first requirement the inequality is 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 7 >= 10 which simplifies to 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 17 >= 0 and testing values between 3 and 6 shows that at x=3 the result is 2(27)-5(9)+3(3)-17 = 54-45+9-17 = 1 which is less than 10 so the condition fails at the lower bound while at x=4 the result is 2(64)-5(16)+3(4)-17 = 128-80+12-17 = 43 which satisfies the condition and since the function is increasing in this interval the valid range starts just above 3 and continues to 6 so the range of x-values that satisfy the condition is approximately x > 3.2 within the given bounds
******
to find the screen width where the area is 15 we solve 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 7 = 15 which becomes 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 22 = 0 and by testing integer values we find that x=3 gives 2(27)-5(9)+3(3)-22 = 54-45+9-22 = -4 and x=4 gives 2(64)-5(16)+3(4)-22 = 128-80+12-22 = 38 so the solution lies between 3 and 4 and using numerical methods the exact width is approximately 3.36 inches while the other two roots are complex numbers which are not physically meaningful for screen width
